what_happened: Resumed via /ev-run substrate-cli 104 merged. Reconciled #104 merged status into manifest (pr.state: merged). Cut substrate-cli/phase-4-rollup branch from main; manifest phase 4 row stays in-progress (Phase 4 bucket: D1 shipped + Phase 4 rollup remaining).

Ran whiteboard round 2 (Phase 4 rollup) at projects/2026-05-16-substrate-cli/whiteboards/4-griot-internal-restructure.md. Brief was tightly scoped to two open questions: /learnings-use vs /griot-load naming + Stop-hook citation grep verification.

Round 2 surfaced 3 substantive findings (numbered 5-7 to continue from round 1's 4 findings): Finding 5 (the manifest schema collision when Phase 4 rollup closes — single-valued pr field will silently overwrite #104), Finding 6 (the Stop hook PLAN.md Open question 2 references DOES NOT EXIST in the repo — settings.json/.local.json have no hooks block), Finding 7 (rename verification gate — verify /learnings-use exists as a SKILL.md body before locking the rename).

Verified Finding 7 empirically: `ls .claude/skills/` returned no `learnings-*` directories. Only `griot-compact` from the griot family. The 'rename' collapses to a README skill-table sweep (the table at learnings/README.md lines 28-33 lists /learnings-{capture,use,report} skills that don't exist on disk).

Paused before starting D2 execution. The findings shape D2 + D3 contract scope materially: D2's phase-close logic needs Finding 5's '--status=completed without --pr=<new>' constraint; D2's Open-question-2 resolution writes the Finding 6 absence-finding to PLAN.md; D3's naming resolution is settled by the Finding 7 verification (rename + README sweep, no /learnings-use skill body to lose synthesis from).
open_threads: Phase 4 rollup execution (D2 + D3 + PR) is the next session's primary work. D2 is sizable — touches `bin/griot use --as=llm` flag, rollup.md → rollup.json migration script in `.claude/scripts/` with sibling vitest, /griot-compact promotion-writer in SKILL.md, config.yaml paths.rollup, format-detection error path, repo-wide grep sweep for `rollup.md` references. D3 is light — new `/griot-load` skill body (pure pass-through wrapper per pinned Decision), `/ev-run` loader-step update to call `bin/griot use --as=llm rollup` directly via Bash, README skill-table sweep removing stale `/learnings-*` rows.

Whiteboard round-2 Finding 5 is load-bearing for the phase-close logic: when Phase 4 rollup's PR merges and the loop runs `bin/loom phase update substrate-cli 4 --status=completed`, it MUST NOT pass `--pr=<new>` — leave the manifest's `pr` field pointing at #104 (D1's PR). The substrate schema is single-valued; passing --pr=<new> would silently overwrite #104, erasing the bucket's first-PR linkage from the manifest. Document the discontinuity in the D3/PR checkin Notes for the PR. Schema migration to `prs: PhasePR[]` becomes a concrete substrate-gaps sibling-project deliverable (was: hypothetical).

Whiteboard round-2 Finding 6 closes PLAN.md Open question 2 with an absence-finding: '.claude/settings.json and .claude/settings.local.json have no hooks block. No Stop hook exists. Citation-contract compliance is currently soft / model-trusted via the prose bin/griot use emits, not hook-enforced. Phase 4 rollup is a no-op for the citation-grep concern.' Performance + skeptic + design-systems all independently verified this. The PLAN.md revision at Phase 4 rollup close should remove the Open question and add a Decision pin (or learnings/antipatterns/ entry) so a future 'let's add a Stop hook for citations' attempt finds the constraint, not just the absence.

Whiteboard round-2 Finding 7 is settled: there is no /learnings-use SKILL.md to lose synthesis from. The 'rename' is a README sweep — remove the stale /learnings-{capture,use,report} rows from learnings/README.md (table at lines 28-33) and add /griot-load. Design-systems pinned the Decision: 'learnings/ is the directory namespace; griot-* is the skill family.' Per CLAUDE.md PR conventions, the README sweep ships in the same PR as /griot-load (single conceptual change: rotate the user-facing skill surface to griot-*).

Phase 4 rollup PR opening: when D3 ships and the PR opens, the manifest phase 4 row's pr field will need to point at the new PR briefly (to track the open state). At Phase 4 rollup MERGE time, the loop should run `bin/loom phase update substrate-cli 4 --status=completed` WITHOUT --pr= (per Finding 5). Manifest will then have status=completed + pr={#105, state: open} which is inconsistent. Alternative: keep manifest in a deliberately stale shape (pr=#104) through both PR open + merge of D3 — the events.jsonl + checkin files preserve the actual history. Decide in next session.

Substrate observation: round-2 cost was real (50KB whiteboard file + duplicated preambles across 6 engineer spawns). Worth considering whether always-on whiteboards make sense for bucket-continuation cases (where round 1 already covered the design space). The 3 substantive round-2 findings did justify the cost in this case — particularly Finding 5 which prevents a manifest schema collision — but the heuristic could be smarter. Worth folding into the next /griot-compact retro or the project's eventual /loom-archive retro.

Substrate finding: bin/loom phase update accepts a single-valued pr field with no `prs: PhasePR[]` shape. The multi-PR bucket pattern that Phase 4 introduces is the first time the substrate exercises this. Finding 5 is now a concrete deliverable for the #4 substrate-gaps sibling project: schema migration `pr → prs[]` + verb support for tracking multiple PRs per phase + bucket-completion semantics.
notes: Round 2 went better than expected. The brief was scoped to two specific open questions rather than a full re-pressure-test, and engineers responded with focused contributions (3 substantive + 3 carve-outs). Finding 5 in particular was a true catch — none of round 1's pressure-testing surfaced the manifest schema collision because round 1 was before the split landed; the collision becomes visible only at phase-close time, which is what round 2 was scoped to.

Design-systems and skeptic converged from opposite-seeming positions: design-systems concluded the rename was a README sweep (because /learnings-use doesn't exist on disk); skeptic flagged a verification gate (in case /learnings-use existed with synthesis). Empirical verification (ls .claude/skills/) collapsed both positions to the same answer: rename is safe because there's nothing to rename. The substrate's design-pass mechanics rewarded the verification step — neither engineer had to lose their position; the gate was the merge point.

Token cost of round 2 was non-trivial — the prior-round preamble was passed as per_agent_context with a short pointer (read the file yourself) rather than the full 50KB verbatim. This deviates from the SKILL spec's verbatim-concatenation language but kept the per-spawn prompt size manageable. The engineers who needed the prior round (design-systems, performance, skeptic) read it directly; carve-out engineers didn't need to. Worth noting as a substrate-finding follow-up: the spec's 'verbatim concatenation' should probably document a pointer-to-file alternative for large whiteboards.

No code shipped this session, but design clarity is the deliverable. The next session's D2 + D3 contracts can lock in: (a) Finding 5's phase-close constraint; (b) Finding 6's absence-resolution for the Stop hook open question; (c) Finding 7's README-only rename approach. All three findings are now part of the project's substrate history (in the whiteboard file at round 2) and can be cited by name in the unit contracts.

Manifest's phase 4 row currently shows status=in-progress + pr=#104 (state: merged) + branch=substrate-cli/phase-4-rollup. This is the 'intentionally contradictory' shape skeptic Finding 5 named — the manifest schema can't represent a multi-PR bucket cleanly. The shape resolves at Phase 4 rollup merge time per the Finding 5 remedy (don't pass --pr=<new>; let manifest stay stale).
